commands.review_summary:
The PR updates `src/daemon/systemd.ts` and `src/daemon/systemd.test.ts` to select a single owner for same-name user/system gateway units, retire live opposite-scope conflicts, and cover the handoff cases with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +168, Tests +368. Total +536 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection shows current main prefers the user unit when both same-name scopes exist, and the linked issue plus PR proof describe the resulting dueling-service restart loop. I did not run live systemd locally in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair lane is needed from this review; remaining action is normal maintainer merge gating and acceptance of the documented service-management risk.

target.title: Fix systemd gateway unit scope conflicts
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #79375.

- Detect same-name user-scope and system-scope systemd gateway units and prefer the active/enabled system unit instead of blindly selecting the user unit.
- Retire only live opposite-scope conflicts, and stage/preflight the replacement user unit before stopping a system-owned gateway during install.
- Add regression coverage for conflict detection, safe install handoff ordering, inactive system-unit files, activation rollback, and restart-time user-unit retirement.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The systemd gateway scope resolver treated a user-scope unit file as authoritative whenever it existed, even if a same-name system-scope OpenClaw gateway unit was active or enabled.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch changes the owner decision for the conflicting-unit state and retires the stale opposite-scope unit at the activation/control boundary, so OpenClaw no longer starts or restarts two default gateway services against the same port.

## Scope and source-of-truth boundary

- **What did NOT change:** This PR only changes OpenClaw-managed systemd gateway unit ownership when the same resolved service name exists in both user and system scopes. It does not change Telegram message handling, Telegram polling/webhook behavior, stale PID matching, launchd/schtasks service management, or the documented multi-gateway pattern of using separate unit names, ports, configs, and state directories.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** `findInstalledSystemdGatewayScope()` is the canonical owner selector for gateway systemd control paths. When a same-name system unit is active or enabled, the system unit owns the gateway and the stale user unit is treated as the conflict; otherwise the user unit remains the owner. Install/control boundaries retire the opposite-scope conflict before activation or restart/stop.
- **Related open PR scan:** The issue-linked PR scan returned no open PRs, and the public-contract related-PR scan returned no related open PRs for the systemd unit/config terms.
- **Out of scope:** This does not introduce a new config key, schema shape, migration, or Telegram-specific recovery path. Existing hosts that intentionally run multiple gateways should keep doing so with distinct service names and ports instead of two same-name units.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Upgrades or repairs that leave same-name user-scope and system-scope OpenClaw gateway units no longer let both services keep managing the same gateway port.
- **Behavior addressed:** Same as above; this uses the exact short label expected by the contributor PR template.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux systemd 239 host with real temporary `sleep 600` systemd units. No Telegram token was used or printed; the install proof used only a dummy gateway token and redacted it from the command transcript.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Created real active/enabled same-name system and user units, then ran `OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT=openclaw-proof-79375-restart pnpm openclaw gateway restart --force`.
  2. Created a real active/enabled system unit with no user unit, then ran `OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT=openclaw-proof-79375-install-order pnpm openclaw gateway install --force --port 19789 --token [dummy-redacted]` from the patched checkout.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal captures from the patched CLI:

  ```text
  ## restart conflict proof
  ## before patched CLI restart
  system active=active
  system enabled=enabled
  user active=active
  user enabled=enabled
  system unit file exists=yes
  user unit file exists=yes

  ## patched CLI command
  OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT=openclaw-proof-79375-restart pnpm openclaw gateway restart --force
  Retired conflicting systemd service: openclaw-proof-79375-restart.service (/home/0668001030/.config/systemd/user/openclaw-proof-79375-restart.service)
  Gateway restart failed: Error: openclaw-proof-79375-restart.service is a system-scope unit (/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-proof-79375-restart.service); run `sudo systemctl restart openclaw-proof-79375-restart.service` to restart it
  cli_exit=1

  ## after patched CLI restart
  system active=active
  system enabled=enabled
  user active=inactive
  user enabled=Failed to get unit file state for openclaw-proof-79375-restart.service: No such file or directory
  system unit file exists=yes
  user unit file exists=no

  ## install-order proof
  ## before patched CLI install
  system active=active
  system enabled=enabled
  user active=inactive
  user enabled=Failed to get unit file state for openclaw-proof-79375-install-order.service: No such file or directory
  system unit file exists=yes
  user unit file exists=no

  ## patched CLI command
  OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT=openclaw-proof-79375-install-order pnpm openclaw gateway install --force --port 19789 --token [dummy-redacted]
  Gateway install failed: Error: system-scope openclaw-proof-79375-install-order.service already exists at /etc/systemd/system/openclaw-proof-79375-install-order.service; run `sudo systemctl disable --now openclaw-proof-79375-install-order.service` before installing the user service: Failed to disable unit: 连接超时
  cli_exit=1

  ## after patched CLI install
  system active=active
  system enabled=enabled
  user active=inactive
  user enabled=disabled
  system unit file exists=yes
  user unit file exists=yes

  ## cleanup
  system unit file exists=no
  user unit file exists=no
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** Restart selected the active system-scope owner and removed the stale user unit before refusing non-root system restart. Install left an active/enabled system owner running when the non-root CLI could not retire it; the replacement user unit was staged but remained inactive/disabled, so the command did not create a dueling running gateway.
- **What was not tested:** Live Telegram bot response was not exercised in this shell; this proof targets the underlying systemd service conflict that caused Telegram-visible gateway outages in the issue.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/systemd.test.ts`
  - pass: 1 test file passed, 94 tests passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
  - pass: no TypeScript errors reported.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/daemon/systemd.ts src/daemon/systemd.test.ts`
  - pass: both changed files use the correct format.
- `git diff --check`
  - pass: no whitespace errors reported.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/daemon/systemd.test.ts`
- **Fix classification:** root-cause systemd service conflict fix
- **Scenario locked in:** The tests cover both same-name scopes existing at once: active/enabled system unit wins and carries the stale user unit as `conflictingUnit`; inactive/disabled system unit files do not force retirement; user install writes and reloads the replacement before stopping a live system conflict; user preflight failure leaves the system unit untouched; replacement activation failure disables the user unit and restores the retired system unit; system restart retires a stale user unit before restarting the system unit.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The tests assert owner selection, live-conflict gating, systemctl command ordering, rollback commands, user-unit unlinking, and the user-visible `Retired conflicting systemd service` output so a future change cannot silently reintroduce user-first ownership, broad inactive-unit retirement, or no-owner install failures.
- **Patch quality notes:** The diff stays in the systemd service owner and its tests; it does not change Telegram message handling, stale PID matching, config schema, or launchd/schtasks service management.

## Merge risk

- **Why it matters / User impact:** A dueling user/system service pair can keep killing the gateway, causing channels such as Telegram to stop responding and to hit API rate limits during repeated restarts.
- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk:service-management`, `merge-risk:linux-systemd`
- **Risk explanation:** The change stops/disables only live conflicting same-name systemd units. Inactive/disabled system unit files are left alone, user install preflight failures leave a running system owner untouched, and replacement activation failures attempt to restore the retired system unit.
- **Why acceptable:** The default `openclaw-gateway.service` unit represents one managed gateway. Keeping two same-name units alive is the failure mode in this issue, while the install path now avoids broad inactive-unit retirement and avoids stopping the system owner before the user replacement has been staged and the user manager has accepted daemon reload.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** live systemd conflict proof, focused unit tests, typecheck, format, and diff checks pass. Live Telegram response was not exercised because the verified failure mode is the underlying gateway service conflict.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
